content:
# Tasks Document

## References

- **Issue:** FORGE-5
- **Spec Path:** `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/`

## File Touch Map

| Action | File | Scope |
|--------|------|-------|
| MODIFY | `src/types/index.ts` | Add `globalVariables` to View, `ParsedVariables` interface, `globalVariableValues` to GeneratedConfig, extend SelectedNode |
| MODIFY | `src/lib/template-parser.ts` | `parseVariables()` returns `ParsedVariables { local, global }` |
| MODIFY | `src/lib/substitution-engine.ts` | `generateConfig()` accepts optional `globalValues` parameter |
| MODIFY | `src/lib/syntax-highlighter.ts` | Split `variable` token into `variable-global` and `variable` (local) |
| MODIFY | `src/store/index.ts` | Add global variable CRUD actions, `autoSyncGlobals`, update `importData` merge logic |
| MODIFY | `src/components/Sidebar.tsx` | Add "Global Variables" TreeNode at depth 1 under each View |
| MODIFY | `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx` | Global vars section in right sidebar, promote-to-global action, green highlight overlay |
| MODIFY | `src/components/ConfigGenerator.tsx` | Pass global values to `generateConfig()`, capture on save |
| MODIFY | `src/components/VariableForm.tsx` | Add global variables info card section |
| MODIFY | `src/components/SaveGeneratedModal.tsx` | Capture `globalVariableValues` on save |
| MODIFY | `src/components/GeneratedConfigViewer.tsx` | Display `globalVariableValues` section |
| CREATE | `src/components/GlobalVariablesPage.tsx` | Full CRUD management page for View-scoped global variables |
| TEST | `src/lib/__tests__/template-parser.test.ts` | Verify discriminated parse output |
| TEST | `src/lib/__tests__/substitution-engine.test.ts` | Verify global/local resolution |

## UI Prototype Gate

> **BLOCKING:** Tasks 0.1–0.3 MUST be completed and approved before ANY task tagged `ui:true` begins.

- [ ] 0.1 Create visual mockup for GlobalVariablesPage and editor sidebar globals section
  - Invoke the `ui-mockup` skill or `frontend-design` skill
  - Cover: GlobalVariablesPage (populated, empty state), editor right sidebar globals section, Generate view info card
  - Use Forge dark theme: slate backgrounds, amber accent for local vars, green accent for global vars
  - Purpose: Establish visual direction before writing any UI code
  - _Requirements: 3, 4, 5, 6_
  - _Prompt: Role: UI/UX Designer | Task: Create visual mockups for the FORGE-5 Global Variables feature using the ui-mockup skill (Stitch) or frontend-design skill. Cover three areas: (1) GlobalVariablesPage — full CRUD page with variable list showing name, value input, type selector, mask toggle, description, reorder handles; include populated and empty states. (2) TemplateEditor right sidebar — new "Global Variables" collapsible section above local variables, showing read-only global var entries with green accent and "Manage" button. (3) ConfigGenerator VariableForm — info card at top showing global variable values (read-only) with "Manage Global Variables" link and "Not set" warnings. Use Forge dark theme (slate backgrounds, forge-charcoal, forge-graphite, amber for local vars, green for global vars, Inter font, JetBrains Mono for values). Reference existing VariableDetectionPanel visual patterns. | Restrictions: Do NOT write any production code. Output is mockup artifacts only. Save to `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/`. | Success: Mockups cover all three areas with populated and empty states, using Forge dark theme._

- [ ] 0.2 Build interactive prototype (Playground)
  - Invoke the `playground` skill using the approved mockup as spec
  - Must use Forge's actual tech patterns: dark theme, Tailwind classes, Lucide icons
  - Include realistic sample data (enable_password, ntp_server, snmp_community as globals)
  - Purpose: Validate UX feel and interactions before production code
  - _Requirements: 3, 4, 5, 6_
  - _Prompt: Role: Frontend Prototyper | Task: Build an interactive single-file HTML playground using the playground skill. Source visual design from the approved mockup (Task 0.1). Simulate: (1) GlobalVariablesPage with add/edit/delete/reorder/mask-toggle for globals. (2) Editor sidebar showing global vs local variable split with green/amber color coding. (3) Generate view info card showing global values read-only. Use Forge dark theme (slate/charcoal backgrounds, amber for local, green for global, Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts). Include realistic network config data: ${enable_password}, ${ntp_server}, ${snmp_community} as globals, $hostname, $mgmt_ip as locals. Save to `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`. | Restrictions: Throwaway prototype — do NOT integrate into codebase. | Success: User can interact with all three areas in a browser and validate the UX._

- [ ] 0.3 Visual approval checkpoint
  - Present prototype to user for explicit approval
  - Update design.md `Prototype Artifacts` section with artifact paths
  - Purpose: Hard gate — no UI implementation proceeds without visual sign-off
  - _Requirements: 3, 4, 5, 6_
  - _Prompt: Role: Project Coordinator | Task: Present the interactive prototype (Task 0.2) to the user. Ask explicitly — does this look and feel right? Collect approval or revision feedback. If approved, update the UI Impact Assessment section in design.md with the playground file path in Prototype Artifacts. | Restrictions: Do NOT proceed to any ui:true implementation task until the user explicitly approves the prototype. | Success: User has approved the visual design. design.md Prototype Artifacts section is populated._

---

- [ ] 1. Extend type definitions for global variables
  - **Files:** `src/types/index.ts`
  - Add `globalVariables: VariableDefinition[]` and `masked?: boolean` to View interface
  - Add `ParsedVariables` interface: `{ local: VariableDefinition[], global: string[] }`
  - Add `globalVariableValues?: Record<string, string>` to GeneratedConfig interface
  - Extend SelectedNode union with `{ type: 'globalVariables'; viewId: string }`
  - Purpose: Establish type foundation for the entire feature
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/types/index.ts`_
  - _Requirements: 1, 2, 7_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript Developer | Task: Extend the type definitions in `src/types/index.ts` for the Global Variables feature. (1) Add `globalVariables: VariableDefinition[]` to the `View` interface. (2) Add `masked?: boolean` to the `VariableDefinition` interface. (3) Create a new `ParsedVariables` interface with `{ local: VariableDefinition[], global: string[] }` and export it. (4) Add `globalVariableValues?: Record<string, string>` to the `GeneratedConfig` interface. (5) If a `SelectedNode` type or equivalent selection state type exists, extend it to include `{ type: 'globalVariables'; viewId: string }`. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify any existing field semantics. All additions must be backward-compatible (use optional `?` where existing data won't have the field). Do NOT add runtime code — types only. | Success: All new types compile without errors. Existing code still compiles without changes (backward compatible). The `ParsedVariables` interface is exported._

- [ ] 2. Update parser to return discriminated local/global results
  - **Files:** `src/lib/template-parser.ts`
  - Change `parseVariables()` return type from `VariableDefinition[]` to `ParsedVariables`
  - Match group 2 (braced `${var}`) → `global` array, match group 3 (bare `$var`) → `local` array
  - If same name appears as both `$foo` and `${foo}`, put in `global` only
  - Purpose: Core detection logic that all UI and generation depends on
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/lib/template-parser.ts`, `src/types/index.ts`_
  - _Requirements: 1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript Developer | Task: Update `parseVariables()` in `src/lib/template-parser.ts` to return the new `ParsedVariables` type instead of `VariableDefinition[]`. The existing regex already has dual capture groups — group 2 for `${var}` (braced) and group 3 for `$var` (bare). Separate matches: braced matches go to `global` as string names, bare matches go to `local` as full `VariableDefinition` objects (using existing `inferType()` and `toLabel()` helpers). If the same variable name appears as both `$foo` and `${foo}`, the braced form wins — put it in `global` only, not in `local`. Deduplicate both arrays. | Restrictions: Do NOT change the regex pattern itself. Do NOT modify `parseSections()`, `cleanUpSections()`, or `rebuildRawText()`. Keep the Cisco type-9 hash stripping logic. Export the `ParsedVariables` type from this file too (re-export from types). | Success: `parseVariables('$local_var ${global_var}')` returns `{ local: [VariableDefinition for local_var], global: ['global_var'] }`. Mixed `$foo ${foo}` returns only in `global`._

- [ ] 3. Update all parseVariables callers to handle new return type
  - **Files:** `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`, any other callers
  - Update destructuring from `parseVariables()` to use `{ local, global }` result
  - Wire global names to `autoSyncGlobals` store action (created in Task 4)
  - Purpose: Adapt existing code to the new parser interface
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`, `src/lib/template-parser.ts`_
  - _Requirements: 1, 5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Developer | Task: Find all callers of `parseVariables()` in the codebase (primarily `TemplateEditor.tsx` and any other files). Update each call site to destructure the new `ParsedVariables` return type: `const { local, global } = parseVariables(text)`. In `TemplateEditor.tsx`: (1) Use `local` array where `variables` was previously used (for the VariableDetectionPanel, variable section map, etc.). (2) Store `global` names in component state for display in the editor right sidebar. (3) Call `autoSyncGlobals(viewId, global)` from the store to auto-register detected globals on the View. The `viewId` should come from the current selection context. | Restrictions: Do NOT create the `autoSyncGlobals` store action (that's Task 4). If it doesn't exist yet, add a TODO comment. Do NOT change VariableDetectionPanel props — it still receives `VariableDefinition[]` (the `local` array). Preserve the 300ms debounce behavior. | Success: All `parseVariables()` call sites compile and work with the new return type. Local variables display unchanged. Global variable names are available in component state._

- [ ] 4. Add global variable store actions
  - **Files:** `src/store/index.ts`
  - Add actions: `addGlobalVariable`, `updateGlobalVariable`, `deleteGlobalVariable`, `reorderGlobalVariables`, `autoSyncGlobals`
  - Initialize `globalVariables: []` on new Views in `addView`
  - Update `importData` to merge global variables by name (additive)
  - Purpose: Complete CRUD and lifecycle management for global variables
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/store/index.ts`, `src/types/index.ts`_
  - _Requirements: 2, 8_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React/Zustand Developer | Task: Add global variable management actions to the Zustand store in `src/store/index.ts`. (1) `addGlobalVariable(viewId, variable: VariableDefinition)` — append to View's `globalVariables` array, update `updatedAt`. (2) `updateGlobalVariable(viewId, name, updates: Partial<VariableDefinition>)` — find by name and merge updates. (3) `deleteGlobalVariable(viewId, name)` — filter out by name. (4) `reorderGlobalVariables(viewId, orderedNames: string[])` — reorder array to match given name order. (5) `autoSyncGlobals(viewId, detectedNames: string[])` — for each name not already in View's globals, auto-add a new VariableDefinition with type 'string', empty defaultValue, auto-generated label (use `toLabel` pattern). Do NOT remove globals that aren't in detectedNames. (6) Update `addView` to initialize `globalVariables: []`. (7) Update `importData` to merge globalVariables by name: imported values update existing, new names added, existing names not in import preserved. | Restrictions: Follow existing store patterns (`set((state) => { ... })`). Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for IDs if needed. Use `new Date().toISOString()` for timestamps. Do NOT modify the persist middleware configuration — the new View field is automatically persisted. | Success: All 5 actions work correctly. New Views have empty `globalVariables`. Import merges globals additively._

- [ ] 5. Update substitution engine for global variable resolution
  - **Files:** `src/lib/substitution-engine.ts`
  - Add optional `globalValues?: Record<string, string>` parameter to `generateConfig()`
  - In `substituteVariables()`: resolve `${var}` from globalValues first, then `$var` from localValues
  - Purpose: Core generation logic for global variable substitution
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/lib/substitution-engine.ts`_
  - _Requirements: 7_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript Developer | Task: Update the substitution engine in `src/lib/substitution-engine.ts`. (1) Add optional `globalValues?: Record<string, string>` parameter to `generateConfig()` with default `{}`. (2) Pass `globalValues` through to `substituteVariables()`. (3) In `substituteVariables()`, perform two-pass replacement: FIRST replace `${variable}` patterns using `globalValues[variable]` (leave unchanged if missing/empty), THEN replace `$variable` patterns using `localValues[variable]` (existing behavior). The order matters — `${var}` must resolve from globals before the `$var` pass runs. | Restrictions: The `globalValues` parameter MUST be optional with default `{}` so all existing callers continue to work without changes. Do NOT change the replacement behavior for unfilled variables (leave as-is). Do NOT modify the `GeneratedConfigOutput` type. | Success: `generateConfig(sections, { hostname: 'SW1' }, { enable_password: 'secret' })` resolves `$hostname` → 'SW1' and `${enable_password}` → 'secret'. Calling without `globalValues` works identically to before._

- [ ] 6. Update syntax highlighter for global vs local variable colors `ui:true`
  - **Files:** `src/lib/syntax-highlighter.ts`, `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`
  - Split `VARIABLE_RE` detection into `variable-global` (for `${...}`) and `variable` (for `$...`) token classes
  - Update all format tokenizers (CLI, XML, JSON, YAML)
  - Update TemplateEditor overlay to apply green background for `${var}` and amber for `$var`
  - Purpose: Visual distinction between global and local variables in the editor
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/lib/syntax-highlighter.ts`, `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`, `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`_
  - _Requirements: 5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task. Source your visual design from the prototype file at `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`. Role: Frontend Developer | Task: Update the syntax highlighter and editor overlay to visually distinguish global from local variables. (1) In `src/lib/syntax-highlighter.ts`: update the variable regex matching in ALL format tokenizers (tokenizeCli, tokenizeXml, tokenizeJson, tokenizeYaml) to emit className `'variable-global'` for `${...}` matches and keep `'variable'` for `$...` matches. (2) In `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`: update the `highlightedText` memo overlay to apply `bg-green-500/25 text-green-300` for `${var}` patterns and keep `bg-amber-500/25 text-amber-300` for `$var` patterns. The overlay regex split should distinguish the two forms. | Restrictions: Do NOT change the underlying regex patterns for what constitutes a valid variable name. Only change the token classification and CSS classes. | Success: In the editor, `${enable_password}` appears with a green highlight and `$hostname` appears with an amber highlight._

- [ ] 7. Add Global Variables sidebar tree node `ui:true`
  - **Files:** `src/components/Sidebar.tsx`
  - Add TreeNode at depth 1 between View and first Vendor with Globe icon
  - Show count badge when globals exist
  - On click: set selection state to `{ type: 'globalVariables', viewId }`
  - Purpose: Navigation entry point for Global Variables management
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/Sidebar.tsx`, `src/components/TreeNode.tsx`, `src/store/index.ts`, `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`_
  - _Requirements: 3_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task. Source your visual design from the prototype file at `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`. Role: React Developer | Task: Add a "Global Variables" tree node to the sidebar in `src/components/Sidebar.tsx`. (1) Import `Globe` from lucide-react. (2) Inside each View's tree rendering, AFTER the View node and BEFORE the vendor loop, add a `<TreeNode>` with: `id={viewId + '__globals'}`, `label="Global Variables"`, `icon={<Globe size={14} />}`, `depth={1}`, `hasChildren={false}`. (3) Read the View's `globalVariables` array from the store to show a count badge (e.g., "(3)") next to the label when globals exist. (4) On select: update the selection state so the main content area renders `GlobalVariablesPage` for this View. Use whatever selection pattern exists (check how vendor/model/variant selection works and follow the same pattern). | Restrictions: Do NOT create GlobalVariablesPage (that's Task 8). Just wire the selection state so it can be conditionally rendered later. Do NOT add edit/delete/duplicate actions to this node. | Success: "Global Variables" appears in the sidebar under each View with a Globe icon. Clicking it updates the selection state. Count badge shows when globals exist._

- [ ] 8. Create GlobalVariablesPage component `ui:true`
  - **Files:** `src/components/GlobalVariablesPage.tsx` (CREATE)
  - Full CRUD management page: list globals with name, value, type, mask toggle, description
  - Add, edit, delete, reorder functionality
  - Empty state messaging
  - Purpose: Primary management interface for global variables
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/VariableDetectionPanel.tsx`, `src/components/VariableInput.tsx`, `src/store/index.ts`, `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`_
  - _Requirements: 4_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task. Source your visual design from the prototype file at `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`. The prototype IS the approved design. Role: React Developer | Task: Create `src/components/GlobalVariablesPage.tsx` — a full CRUD management page for View-scoped global variables. Props: `{ viewId: string }`. (1) Read global variables from store: `tree.views.find(v => v.id === viewId)?.globalVariables`. (2) List each global with: name (editable), value input (using type-appropriate input like VariableInput patterns), type selector dropdown (string, ip, integer, dropdown), mask toggle (switches input type between text/password, reads from `masked` field), description (editable). (3) Add button: appends new global with auto-generated name, type 'string', empty value. (4) Delete button per variable (with visual confirmation). (5) Reorder: drag handles or up/down buttons to reorder globals. Use store actions: `addGlobalVariable`, `updateGlobalVariable`, `deleteGlobalVariable`, `reorderGlobalVariables`. (6) Empty state: "No global variables yet. Use \`\${variable_name}\` syntax in your templates to auto-detect globals, or add one manually." (7) Style with Forge dark theme: `bg-forge-charcoal`, `text-slate-200`, green accent for global variable indicators. Follow VariableDetectionPanel visual patterns (accordion expand, inline editing). | Restrictions: Use existing store actions (Task 4). Do NOT duplicate store logic. Follow existing component patterns (functional component, hooks). | Success: Page displays all global variables with full CRUD. Mask toggle works. Empty state shows helpful message. Changes persist via store._

- [ ] 9. Wire GlobalVariablesPage into main content area `ui:true`
  - **Files:** `src/App.tsx` or main layout component
  - Conditionally render GlobalVariablesPage when selection type is 'globalVariables'
  - Purpose: Connect sidebar navigation to the new page
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/App.tsx`, `src/components/Sidebar.tsx`, `src/components/GlobalVariablesPage.tsx`_
  - _Requirements: 3_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: React Developer | Task: Wire the GlobalVariablesPage into the main content rendering in `src/App.tsx` (or whatever component handles the main content area based on sidebar selection). Find where the app conditionally renders different content based on selection state (TemplateEditor, ConfigGenerator, GeneratedConfigViewer, etc.) and add a case for `type: 'globalVariables'` that renders `<GlobalVariablesPage viewId={selectedNode.viewId} />`. | Restrictions: Follow the existing conditional rendering pattern exactly. Do NOT refactor the routing/selection system. | Success: Clicking "Global Variables" in the sidebar shows the GlobalVariablesPage in the main content area._

- [ ] 10. Add global variables section to editor right sidebar `ui:true`
  - **Files:** `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`
  - Add collapsible "Global Variables" section above the existing variables panel
  - Show detected globals with green accent, read-only, current value or "Not set"
  - Add "Promote to Global" button on local variables
  - Purpose: In-context global variable awareness while editing templates
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/TemplateEditor.tsx`, `src/components/VariableDetectionPanel.tsx`, `src/store/index.ts`, `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`_
  - _Requirements: 5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task. Source your visual design from the prototype file at `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`. Role: React Developer | Task: Update the TemplateEditor right sidebar to show global variables. (1) Add a new collapsible section ABOVE the existing VariableDetectionPanel labeled "Global Variables" with a Globe icon and count badge. (2) For each detected global variable name (from the `global` array in component state, set in Task 3): look up the full definition from the View's `globalVariables` store, display name + current value (or "Not set" in amber). Use green accent color (`text-green-400`). Mark as read-only (no inline editing). (3) Add a "Manage" button that navigates to the GlobalVariablesPage. (4) On each LOCAL variable in the VariableDetectionPanel, add a small "Promote to Global" button (arrow-up-circle icon or similar). On click: find-replace `$varName` → `\${varName}` in the rawText state, which triggers re-parse via debounce, which triggers `autoSyncGlobals`. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify VariableDetectionPanel itself — add the promote button in the parent (TemplateEditor) that wraps or extends the panel display. Keep the existing panel fully functional for local variables. | Success: Editor right sidebar shows global variables section with green accent. "Promote to Global" converts a local var to global syntax. "Manage" button navigates to GlobalVariablesPage._

- [ ] 11. Add global variables info card to Generate view `ui:true`
  - **Files:** `src/components/ConfigGenerator.tsx`, `src/components/VariableForm.tsx`
  - Add info card at top of VariableForm showing global variable values (read-only)
  - Show "Not set" warning for unfilled globals with link to manage page
  - Pass globalValues to `generateConfig()` call
  - Capture `globalVariableValues` when saving to history
  - Purpose: Global variable visibility and resolution in the generation workflow
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/components/ConfigGenerator.tsx`, `src/components/VariableForm.tsx`, `src/components/SaveGeneratedModal.tsx`, `src/store/index.ts`, `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`_
  - _Requirements: 6, 7_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task. Source your visual design from the prototype file at `.spec-workflow/specs/FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var/artifacts/playground.html`. Role: React Developer | Task: Integrate global variables into the Generate view. (1) In `ConfigGenerator.tsx` or `VariableForm.tsx`: add an info card section at the TOP of the variable form, before local variable inputs. Show a Globe icon, "Global Variables" header, and list each global variable with its name and current value (`defaultValue` from the View's globalVariables). If a global has no value (empty `defaultValue`), show "Not set" in amber with a warning icon. Add a "Manage Global Variables" link/button that navigates to GlobalVariablesPage. Style as a distinct info card (darker background, border, read-only appearance). (2) In `ConfigGenerator.tsx`: update the `generateConfig()` call to pass global variable values as the third argument. Build a `Record<string, string>` from the View's `globalVariables` array: `{ [gv.name]: gv.defaultValue }`. (3) In `SaveGeneratedModal.tsx`: when creating the GeneratedConfig object, include `globalVariableValues` — a snapshot of the global values used at generation time. (4) In `GeneratedConfigViewer.tsx`: if `globalVariableValues` exists on the config, display it in a section similar to the local variable values display. | Restrictions: Global values are NOT editable in the Generate view — editing only happens on GlobalVariablesPage. Do NOT add inline edit controls. | Success: Generate view shows global variables info card. Config generation resolves both `${var}` and `$var`. Saved configs capture global values. Viewer shows global values._

- [ ] 12. Update export/import merge logic for global variables
  - **Files:** `src/store/index.ts`
  - Ensure `importData` handles View objects with `globalVariables` field
  - Merge by name: imported values update existing, new names added, existing preserved
  - Handle importing into Views that already have globals
  - Purpose: Complete the data lifecycle for global variables across export/import
  - **Recommended Agent:** Claude
  - _Leverage: `src/store/index.ts`, `src/lib/vault-engine.ts`_
  - _Requirements: 8_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec FORGE-5-global-variables-view-scoped-var, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript Developer | Task: Ensure the store's `importData()` action correctly handles global variables during .stvault import. (1) When importing Views that have `globalVariables`: if the View already exists locally, merge globals by name — imported values overwrite same-named locals, new names are added, existing names NOT in the import are preserved. (2) When importing Views that don't have `globalVariables` (backward compat with old exports): initialize `globalVariables: []` on the imported View. (3) When importing `generatedConfigs` that have `globalVariableValues`: preserve the field. (4) Verify the export path already includes `globalVariables` by checking what `exportData` returns — since it serializes the full View objects, globals should ride along automatically. If not, ensure they're included. | Restrictions: Follow the existing merge pattern in `importData` (which is additive for templates and variable values). Do NOT modify vault-engine.ts — the encryption/decryption layer is format-agnostic. | Success: Export a View with globals → import into fresh state → globals restored. Import into state with existing globals → merge by name._

- [ ] 13. Write unit tests for parser and substitution engine
  - **Files:** `src/lib/__tests__/template-parser.test.ts` (CREATE or MODIFY), `src/lib/__tests__/substitution-engine.test.ts` (CREATE or MODIFY)
